name: garuda (his vahana)
shortDescription: Garuda is a divine bird-like creature and vahana (mount) of the Hindu god Vishnu. He is revered as a powerful protector and destroyer of evil.
description: Garuda is a prominent figure in Hindu mythology, known primarily as the vahana (mount) of Lord Vishnu. He is depicted as a large, majestic bird-like creature, often described as having a golden body, white face, and red wings. Garuda's origins are detailed in various Hindu scriptures, including the Mahabharata and the Puranas. According to these texts, he is the son of Vinata and the sage Kashyapa. A key myth surrounding Garuda involves his quest to obtain Amrita (the nectar of immortality) to free his mother from slavery to his aunt, Kadru, and her serpent sons. This quest highlights his strength, devotion, and unwavering commitment to dharma. Garuda is considered a symbol of power, speed, and bravery. He is also seen as a protector against evil forces and is often invoked for protection and blessings. His image is frequently found adorning temples and is a significant part of Hindu iconography. His association with Vishnu further elevates his importance, as Vishnu is one of the principal deities in the Hindu trinity, representing preservation and cosmic order. Garuda's role as Vishnu's vahana signifies the swiftness and efficiency with which Vishnu upholds dharma and restores balance in the universe. He is also associated with the sun and fire, embodying their radiant and transformative energy.
